title: Long-Term Memory with LangChain and Postgres
tagline: Persist user preferences and facts across threads with LangChain stores backed by Postgres.

story.problem: Thread memory helps inside one conversation, but production agents also need durable user context that survives restarts and new sessions.
story.solution: Use LangChain long-term memory with Postgres-backed LangGraph stores, namespaced by user_id and recalled with tight limits before generation.
story.outcome: A stateful agent that can remember durable user preferences while exposing list and delete flows needed for privacy operations.

quickstart.run: docker compose up -d postgres && uv sync && make dev

endpoints.description: Run the agent with thread_id and user_id, then stream SSE events back.
List the long-term memories stored for a user.
Summarize what the agent knows about a user.
Delete the long-term memories stored for a user.
Delete short-term checkpoints for a conversation thread.
